title: pilosella bauhini subsp. magyarica
intro: Pilosella bauhini subsp. magyarica, also known as Hungarian hawk's beard, is a subspecies of the Pilosella bauhini plant, which belongs to the dandelion family and is native to Europe. Traditionally, Pilosella bauhini subsp. magyarica has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Pilosella bauhini subsp. magyarica is often used in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
